policy.goal: Converge bot-like Telegram runtimes on one Aiogram 3-compatible platform pattern while preserving product behavior through adapters, characterization tests, and explicit exceptions for non-bot surfaces.
policy.role_gate: Every item must route to a plugin-owned specialist role before product or platform implementation. Unknown parts block implementation until the role/catalog artifact is created and validated.
policy.live_runtime_policy: No live Telegram sends, webhook changes, polling changes, production mutation, secrets, raw chats, raw logs, or production payloads without explicit operator approval.
policy.deprecated_projects_refs: Any retained projects/* item is legacy compatibility inventory only and is not canonical project authority; canonical project registry authority is /srv/bears/dev/registry/projects.v1.json.

items.readiness_gate: Product MCP contract, auth tests, MCP smoke, and prod Kubernetes pinned-image rollout evidence required.
next_actions: Keep Telegram MCP behavior in /srv/bears/dev/app/codex-telegram.
Use workspace-control only to register the production MCP endpoint.
Do not create a dev/workspace adapter or local product daemon.
validation_before_code: Run /srv/bears/dev/app/codex-telegram pytest and MCP dry-run smoke.
items.live_runtime_policy: No dev runtime, local product daemon, live Telegram sends, webhook changes, token access, or production mutation without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Readiness packet and test matrix required before shared primitive adoption.
next_actions: Separate reusable Telegram primitives from VPN-only product behavior.
Define callback, RBAC, idempotency, and rendering tests for adapter seams.
validation_before_code: Run nearest vpnbot bot/import and Telegram UI tests for touched flows.
Avoid live bot traffic unless approved.
items.live_runtime_policy: Read-only/import checks only until operator approves any live Telegram mutation.

legacy_compatibility.issue_link_invariant: Old repository issues and migration issues must link to BearsCLOUD/apps before archive.

items.readiness_gate: Project SPEC and requirements plus behavior inventory required before implementation edits.
next_actions: Create minimal project SPEC and requirements before code work.
Record command, callback, allowed-user, Redis FSM, and auth_core behavior inventory.
Add characterization tests around message rendering, callbacks, auth denial, and polling startup/import.
validation_before_code: Run local lint/import/tests from pyproject or documented local command after adding coverage.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live admin bot sends, polling changes, or token access without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Upgrade packet required because the current runtime uses a beta Aiogram 3 dependency.
next_actions: Create minimal project SPEC and requirements before code work.
Inventory commands, FSM states, auth_core checks, Carrot integrations, callbacks, and retry behavior.
Plan beta-to-stable Aiogram upgrade with characterization tests first.
validation_before_code: Validate artifacts, then run local bot import/startup-safe tests and callback/FSM characterization tests.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live polling, sends, or external integration calls without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Aiogram 2 to Aiogram 3 migration packet and behavior tests are mandatory before runtime edits.
next_actions: Create minimal project SPEC and requirements before code work.
Inventory executor-based polling, handlers, marketplace tasks, files, keyboards, and WB-related side effects.
Build Aiogram 2 behavior characterization before Router/Dispatcher/FSM rewrite.
validation_before_code: Validate artifacts, then run or add safe unit/import tests without real Telegram tokens or WB calls.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live bot sends, polling changes, supplier payload access, or WB calls without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Project SPEC and requirements plus behavior inventory required before implementation edits.
next_actions: Create minimal project SPEC and requirements before code work.
Inventory image/sticker generation flow, OpenAI boundary, callback/user input behavior, and async task handoff.
Add rendering and no-secret tests before adapter extraction.
validation_before_code: Validate artifacts, then run local import/tests with mocked OpenAI, Telegram, database, and worker boundaries.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live bot sends, model calls, or production media/payload access without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Stable Aiogram 3 hardening packet and target artifacts required before implementation edits.
next_actions: Create minimal project SPEC and requirements before code work.
Inventory auth binding, main menu, database bootstrap, proxy/session behavior, callbacks, and worker-delivered messages.
Add characterization tests before shared callback/rendering primitive adoption.
validation_before_code: Validate artifacts, then run local bot import/startup-safe tests with mocked Telegram, Redis, DB, and worker boundaries.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live main bot sends, polling changes, DB mutations, or token access without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Notification event and channel-adapter packet required before delivery-service changes.
next_actions: Create minimal project SPEC and requirements before code work.
Map event producers, recipient identity lookup, template rendering, retry/idempotency, and audit fields.
Coordinate with Telegram role only for Telegram-channel formatting and delivery adapter tests.
validation_before_code: Validate artifacts, then run notification handler/API tests with mocked Telegram API and auth responses.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live sends, production recipients, or bot token access without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Inventory-only entry: owner artifacts, product role mapping, and behavior inventory are required before any rewrite planning becomes implementation work.
next_actions: Keep as inventory-only Telegram bot candidate until owning target artifacts and role mapping exist.
Document commands, admin checks, token boundary, and visible bot behavior before any framework rewrite proposal.
Do not infer Aiogram implementation readiness from a single Telegraf file.
validation_before_code: Validate inventory coverage, then require product artifacts and characterization tests before any code work.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live bot sends, token access, or runtime mutation without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Inventory-only non-bot bridge classification: owner artifacts and boundary proof are required before any implementation work.
next_actions: Keep as inventory-only non-bot bridge candidate until owner and artifact coverage are documented.
Verify whether update binding and send-message helpers stay bridge-only or hide a fuller product runtime.
Do not force Aiogram migration without stronger bot-runtime evidence.
validation_before_code: Validate inventory coverage, then require owner artifacts and bridge-boundary tests before code work.
items.live_runtime_policy: No token reads, update polling, or message sends without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Inventory-only non-bot notification classification: owner artifacts and alert-boundary proof are required before implementation work.
next_actions: Keep as inventory-only notification bridge candidate until owner artifacts and sanitized alert boundaries are documented.
Verify whether this helper remains notification-only or expands into a fuller Telegram runtime.
Do not force Aiogram migration for alert delivery helpers.
validation_before_code: Validate inventory coverage, then require owner artifacts and non-live notification tests before code work.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live token use, recipient sends, or runtime mutation without explicit operator approval.

items.current_framework_version: verified by local source scan, exact version not checked in this backlog pass
items.readiness_gate: Collector exception record required before any forced bot-runtime abstraction.
next_actions: Keep as read-only collector adapter; do not force Aiogram bot callback abstractions.
Document session hygiene and read-only evidence boundaries if this surface joins the shared platform.
validation_before_code: Run collector-local tests or import checks with mocked Telegram client/session when code changes.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live account/session actions, collection, or message sends without explicit operator approval.

items.readiness_gate: Dev-core product layer, split proof, adapter seam, and behavior tests are required before any The Ants Telegram runtime rewrite.
next_actions: Keep /srv/bears/projects/theants as deprecated migration/archive evidence and link migration issues to BearsCLOUD/apps before archive.
Route canonical product work through /srv/bears/dev/app plus BearsCLOUD/apps; use /srv/bears/dev/app/theants only as an app directory or migration/archive path.
Use bears-telegram-platform-engineer only as a supporting Telegram pattern reviewer for Aiogram-specific adapter work.
Plan Aiogram 3 dispatcher/router migration only after product behavior is covered by deterministic tests.
validation_before_code: Run Bears platform role route/audit for /srv/bears/dev/app, BearsCLOUD/apps, /srv/bears/dev/app/theants, and /srv/bears/projects/theants.
Run The Ants product and split-proof tests before any Telegram adapter rewrite.
items.live_runtime_policy: No live game/player messages, Telegram calls, Kubernetes migration, production process mutation, secrets, raw chats, raw logs, raw VPN configs, or production payloads without explicit operator approval.
